new_classes.definition_en: A process by which ethyl alcohol or other spirits are rendered unfit for human consumption by the addition of denaturing substances, as required for classification under CN subheading 2207 20 00.

A quality of ethyl alcohol or spirits indicating that the product has been denatured — i.e. rendered unfit for human consumption by the addition of denaturing substances — regardless of its alcoholic strength by volume.

new_classes.label_en: Denatured ethyl alcohol and other spirits, of any strength (CN 2207 20 00)
new_classes.label_de: Ethylalkohol und Branntwein mit beliebigem Alkoholgehalt, vergällt (KN 2207 20 00)
new_classes.definition_en: Ethyl alcohol and other spirits that have been denatured (rendered unfit for human consumption) regardless of their alcoholic strength by volume, classified under CN subheading 2207 20 00. Contrasted with undenatured ethyl alcohol of ≥ 80% vol under CN 2207 10 00.

coverage_explanation: The two primary distinguishing criteria for CN 2207 20 00 are: (1) the product is ethyl alcohol or other spirits, and (2) it has been denatured (i.e. rendered unfit for human consumption by addition of denaturing substances), irrespective of its alcoholic strength by volume. Both criteria are captured via the new DenaturingQuality and DenaturingProcess classes, and a complement restriction distinguishes this class from the sibling undenatured subheading 2207 10 00. Coverage is slightly below 1.0 because: (a) the legal text cross-references the HS Explanatory Note fourth paragraph to heading 2207 which provides further detail on the specific denaturing substances and methods accepted — this detailed technical content cannot be fully axiomatised without additional named quality classes for each accepted denaturing substance or method; (b) the 'any strength' qualifier is noted in the label but is not axiomatised as a formal restriction since it negates a minimum threshold rather than asserts one, and OWL datatype restrictions are less natural for unbounded ranges across the full spectrum — the absence of an alcoholic strength range restriction correctly leaves strength unconstrained.
